verses.latin_vulgate: Erant autem appropinquantes ei publicani, et peccatores ut audirent illum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἦσαν δὲ ἐγγίζοντες αὐτῷ πάντες οἱ τελῶναι καὶ οἱ ἁμαρτωλοὶ ἀκούειν αὐτοῦ
verses.king_james: Then drew near unto him all the publicans and sinners for to hear him.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et murmurabant pharisæi, et scribæ, dicentes: Quia hic peccatores recipit, et manducat cum illis.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ διεγόγγυζον οἵ φαρισαῖοι καὶ οἱ γραμματεῖς λέγοντες ὅτι οὗτος ἁμαρτωλοὺς προσδέχεται καὶ συνεσθίει αὐτοῖς
verses.king_james: And the Pharisees and scribes murmured, saying, This man receiveth sinners, and eateth with them.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et ait ad illos parabolam istam dicens:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: εἶπεν δὲ πρὸς αὐτοὺς τὴν παραβολὴν ταύτην λέγων
verses.king_james: And he spake this parable unto them, saying,

verses.latin_vulgate: Quis ex vobis homo, qui habet centum oves, et si perdiderit unam ex illis, nonne dimittit nonaginta novem in deserto, et vadit ad illam quæ perierat, donec inveniat eam?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: τίς ἄνθρωπος ἐξ ὑμῶν ἔχων ἑκατὸν πρόβατα καὶ ἀπολέσας ἓν ἐξ αὐτῶν οὐ καταλείπει τὰ ἐννενήκονταεννέα ἐν τῇ ἐρήμῳ καὶ πορεύεται ἐπὶ τὸ ἀπολωλὸς ἕως εὕρῃ αὐτό
verses.king_james: What man of you, having an hundred sheep, if he lose one of them, doth not leave the ninety and nine in the wilderness, and go after that which is lost, until he find it?

verses.latin_vulgate: Et cum invenerit eam, imponit in humeros suos gaudens:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ εὑρὼν ἐπιτίθησιν ἐπὶ τοὺς ὤμους ἑαυτοῦ χαίρων
verses.king_james: And when he hath found *it*, he layeth *it* on his shoulders, rejoicing.

verses.latin_vulgate: et veniens domum convocat amicos et vicinos, dicens illis: Congratulamini mihi, quia inveni ovem meam, quæ perierat.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἐλθὼν εἰς τὸν οἶκον συγκαλεῖ τοὺς φίλους καὶ τοὺς γείτονας λέγων αὐτοῖς, συγχάρητέ μοι ὅτι εὗρον τὸ πρόβατόν μου τὸ ἀπολωλός
verses.king_james: And when he cometh home, he calleth together *his* friends and neighbours, saying unto them, Rejoice with me; for I have found my sheep which was lost.

verses.latin_vulgate: Dico vobis quod ita gaudium erit in cælo super uno peccatore pœnitentiam agente, quam super nonaginta novem justis, qui non indigent pœnitentia.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: λέγω ὑμῖν ὅτι οὕτως χαρὰ ἔσται ἐν τῷ οὐρανῷ ἐπὶ ἑνὶ ἁμαρτωλῷ μετανοοῦντι ἢ ἐπὶ ἐννενήκονταεννέα δικαίοις οἵτινες οὐ χρείαν ἔχουσιν μετανοίας
verses.king_james: I say unto you, that likewise joy shall be in heaven over one sinner that repenteth, more than over ninety and nine just persons, which need no repentance.

verses.latin_vulgate: Aut quæ mulier habens drachmas decem, si perdiderit drachmam unam, nonne accendit lucernam, et everrit domum, et quærit diligenter, donec inveniat?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἢ τίς γυνὴ δραχμὰς ἔχουσα δέκα ἐὰν ἀπολέσῃ δραχμὴν μίαν οὐχὶ ἅπτει λύχνον καὶ σαροῖ τὴν οἰκίαν καὶ ζητεῖ ἐπιμελῶς ἕως ὅτου εὕρῃ
verses.king_james: Either what woman having ten pieces of silver, if she lose one piece, doth not light a candle, and sweep the house, and seek diligently till she find *it* ?

verses.latin_vulgate: Et cum invenerit convocat amicas et vicinas, dicens: Congratulamini mihi, quia inveni drachmam quam perdideram.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ εὑροῦσα συγκαλεῖται τὰς φίλας καὶ τὰς γείτονας λέγουσα συγχάρητέ μοι ὅτι εὗρον τὴν δραχμὴν ἣν ἀπώλεσα
verses.king_james: And when she hath found *it*, she calleth *her* friends and *her* neighbours together, saying, Rejoice with me; for I have found the piece which I had lost.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ita, dico vobis, gaudium erit coram angelis Dei super uno peccatore pœnitentiam agente.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: οὕτως λέγω ὑμῖν χαρὰ γίνεται ἐνώπιον τῶν ἀγγέλων τοῦ θεοῦ ἐπὶ ἑνὶ ἁμαρτωλῷ μετανοοῦντι
verses.king_james: Likewise, I say unto you, there is joy in the presence of the angels of God over one sinner that repenteth.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ait autem: Homo quidam habuit duos filios:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: εἶπεν δέ ἄνθρωπός τις εἶχεν δύο υἱούς
verses.king_james: And he said, A certain man had two sons:

verses.latin_vulgate: et dixit adolescentior ex illis patri: Pater, da mihi portionem substantiæ, quæ me contingit. Et divisit illis substantiam.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ εἶπεν ὁ νεώτερος αὐτῶν τῷ πατρί πάτερ δός μοι τὸ ἐπιβάλλον μέρος τῆς οὐσίας καὶ διεῖλεν αὐτοῖς τὸν βίον
verses.king_james: And the younger of them said to *his* father, Father, give me the portion of goods that falleth *to me*. And he divided unto them *his* living.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et non post multos dies, congregatis omnibus, adolescentior filius peregre profectus est in regionem longinquam, et ibi dissipavit substantiam suam vivendo luxuriose.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ μετ᾽ οὐ πολλὰς ἡμέρας συναγαγὼν ἅπαντα ὁ νεώτερος υἱὸς ἀπεδήμησεν εἰς χώραν μακράν καὶ ἐκεῖ διεσκόρπισεν τὴν οὐσίαν αὐτοῦ ζῶν ἀσώτως
verses.king_james: And not many days after the younger son gathered all together, and took his journey into a far country, and there wasted his substance with riotous living.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et postquam omnia consummasset, facta est fames valida in regione illa, et ipse cœpit egere.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: δαπανήσαντος δὲ αὐτοῦ πάντα ἐγένετο λιμὸς ἰσχυρὸς κατὰ τὴν χώραν ἐκείνην καὶ αὐτὸς ἤρξατο ὑστερεῖσθαι
verses.king_james: And when he had spent all, there arose a mighty famine in that land; and he began to be in want.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et abiit, et adhæsit uni civium regionis illius: et misit illum in villam suam ut pasceret porcos.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ πορευθεὶς ἐκολλήθη ἑνὶ τῶν πολιτῶν τῆς χώρας ἐκείνης καὶ ἔπεμψεν αὐτὸν εἰς τοὺς ἀγροὺς αὐτοῦ βόσκειν χοίρους
verses.king_james: And he went and joined himself to a citizen of that country; and he sent him into his fields to feed swine.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et cupiebat implere ventrem suum de siliquis, quas porci manducabant: et nemo illi dabat.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἐπεθύμει γεμίσαι τὴν κοιλίαν αὑτοῦ ἀπὸ τῶν κερατίων ὧν ἤσθιον οἱ χοῖροι καὶ οὐδεὶς ἐδίδου αὐτῷ
verses.king_james: And he would fain have filled his belly with the husks that the swine did eat: and no man gave unto him.

verses.latin_vulgate: In se autem reversus, dixit: Quanti mercenarii in domo patris mei abundant panibus, ego autem hic fame pereo!
verses.greek_textus_receptus: εἰς ἑαυτὸν δὲ ἐλθὼν εἶπεν πόσοι μίσθιοι τοῦ πατρός μου περισσεύουσιν ἄρτων ἐγὼ δὲ λιμῷ ἀπόλλυμαι
verses.king_james: And when he came to himself, he said, How many hired servants of my father's have bread enough and to spare, and I perish with hunger!

verses.latin_vulgate: surgam, et ibo ad patrem meum, et dicam ei: Pater, peccavi in cælum, et coram te:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀναστὰς πορεύσομαι πρὸς τὸν πατέρα μου καὶ ἐρῶ αὐτῷ πάτερ ἥμαρτον εἰς τὸν οὐρανὸν καὶ ἐνώπιόν σου
verses.king_james: I will arise and go to my father, and will say unto him, Father, I have sinned against heaven, and before thee,

verses.latin_vulgate: jam non sum dignus vocari filius tuus: fac me sicut unum de mercenariis tuis.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ οὐκέτι εἰμὶ ἄξιος κληθῆναι υἱός σου ποίησόν με ὡς ἕνα τῶν μισθίων σου
verses.king_james: And am no more worthy to be called thy son: make me as one of thy hired servants.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et surgens venit ad patrem suum. Cum autem adhuc longe esset, vidit illum pater ipsius, et misericordia motus est, et accurrens cecidit super collum ejus, et osculatus est eum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἀναστὰς ἦλθεν πρὸς τὸν πατέρα ἑαυτοῦ ἔτι δὲ αὐτοῦ μακρὰν ἀπέχοντος εἶδεν αὐτὸν ὁ πατὴρ αὐτοῦ καὶ ἐσπλαγχνίσθη καὶ δραμὼν ἐπέπεσεν ἐπὶ τὸν τράχηλον αὐτοῦ καὶ κατεφίλησεν αὐτόν
verses.king_james: And he arose, and came to his father. But when he was yet a great way off, his father saw him, and had compassion, and ran, and fell on his neck, and kissed him.

verses.latin_vulgate: Dixitque ei filius: Pater, peccavi in cælum, et coram te: jam non sum dignus vocari filius tuus.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: εἶπεν δὲ αὐτῷ ὁ υἱὸς πάτερ ἥμαρτον εἰς τὸν οὐρανὸν καὶ ἐνώπιόν σου καὶ οὐκέτι εἰμὶ ἄξιος κληθῆναι υἱός σου
verses.king_james: And the son said unto him, Father, I have sinned against heaven, and in thy sight, and am no more worthy to be called thy son.

verses.latin_vulgate: Dixit autem pater ad servos suos: Cito proferte stolam primam, et induite illum, et date annulum in manum ejus, et calceamenta in pedes ejus:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: εἶπεν δὲ ὁ πατὴρ πρὸς τοὺς δούλους αὐτοῦ ἐξενέγκατε τὴν στολὴν τὴν πρώτην καὶ ἐνδύσατε αὐτόν καὶ δότε δακτύλιον εἰς τὴν χεῖρα αὐτοῦ καὶ ὑποδήματα εἰς τοὺς πόδας
verses.king_james: But the father said to his servants, Bring forth the best robe, and put *it* on him; and put a ring on his hand, and shoes on *his* feet:

verses.latin_vulgate: et adducite vitulum saginatum, et occidite, et manducemus, et epulemur:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἐνέγκαντες τὸν μόσχον τὸν σιτευτόν θύσατε καὶ φαγόντες εὐφρανθῶμεν
verses.king_james: And bring hither the fatted calf, and kill *it*; and let us eat, and be merry:

verses.latin_vulgate: quia hic filius meus mortuus erat, et revixit: perierat, et inventus est. Et cœperunt epulari.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὅτι οὗτος ὁ υἱός μου νεκρὸς ἦν καὶ ἀνέζησεν καὶ ἀπολωλὼς ἦν καὶ εὑρέθη καὶ ἤρξαντο εὐφραίνεσθαι
verses.king_james: For this my son was dead, and is alive again; he was lost, and is found. And they began to be merry.

verses.latin_vulgate: Erat autem filius ejus senior in agro: et cum veniret, et appropinquaret domui, audivit symphoniam et chorum:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἦν δὲ ὁ υἱὸς αὐτοῦ ὁ πρεσβύτερος ἐν ἀγρῷ καὶ ὡς ἐρχόμενος ἤγγισεν τῇ οἰκίᾳ ἤκουσεν συμφωνίας καὶ χορῶν
verses.king_james: Now his elder son was in the field: and as he came and drew nigh to the house, he heard musick and dancing.

verses.latin_vulgate: et vocavit unum de servis, et interrogavit quid hæc essent.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ προσκαλεσάμενος ἕνα τῶν παίδων αὐτοῦ ἐπυνθάνετο τί εἴη ταῦτα
verses.king_james: And he called one of the servants, and asked what these things meant.

verses.latin_vulgate: Isque dixit illi: Frater tuus venit, et occidit pater tuus vitulum saginatum, quia salvum illum recepit.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὁ δὲ εἶπεν αὐτῷ ὅτι ὁ ἀδελφός σου ἥκει καὶ ἔθυσεν ὁ πατήρ σου τὸν μόσχον τὸν σιτευτόν ὅτι ὑγιαίνοντα αὐτὸν ἀπέλαβεν
verses.king_james: And he said unto him, Thy brother is come; and thy father hath killed the fatted calf, because he hath received him safe and sound.

verses.latin_vulgate: Indignatus est autem, et nolebat introire. Pater ergo illius egressus, cœpit rogare illum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὠργίσθη δὲ καὶ οὐκ ἤθελεν εἰσελθεῖν ὁ οὖν πατὴρ αὐτοῦ ἐξελθὼν παρεκάλει αὐτόν
verses.king_james: And he was angry, and would not go in: therefore came his father out, and intreated him.

verses.latin_vulgate: At ille respondens, dixit patri suo: Ecce tot annis servio tibi, et numquam mandatum tuum præterivi: et numquam dedisti mihi hædum ut cum amicis meis epularer.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὁ δὲ ἀποκριθεὶς εἶπεν τῷ πατρὶ ἰδού, τοσαῦτα ἔτη δουλεύω σοι καὶ οὐδέποτε ἐντολήν σου παρῆλθον καὶ ἐμοὶ οὐδέποτε ἔδωκας ἔριφον ἵνα μετὰ τῶν φίλων μου εὐφρανθῶ
verses.king_james: And he answering said to *his* father, Lo, these many years do I serve thee, neither transgressed I at any time thy commandment: and yet thou never gavest me a kid, that I might make merry with my friends:

verses.latin_vulgate: Sed postquam filius tuus hic, qui devoravit substantiam suam cum meretricibus, venit, occidisti illi vitulum saginatum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὅτε δὲ ὁ υἱός σου οὗτος ὁ καταφαγών σου τὸν βίον μετὰ πορνῶν ἦλθεν ἔθυσας αὐτῷ τὸν μόσχον τὸν σιτευτὸν
verses.king_james: But as soon as this thy son was come, which hath devoured thy living with harlots, thou hast killed for him the fatted calf.

verses.latin_vulgate: At ipse dixit illi: Fili, tu semper mecum es, et omnia mea tua sunt:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὁ δὲ εἶπεν αὐτῷ τέκνον σὺ πάντοτε μετ᾽ ἐμοῦ εἶ καὶ πάντα τὰ ἐμὰ σά ἐστιν
verses.king_james: And he said unto him, Son, thou art ever with me, and all that I have is thine.

verses.latin_vulgate: epulari autem, et gaudere oportebat, quia frater tuus hic mortuus erat, et revixit; perierat, et inventus est.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: εὐφρανθῆναι δὲ καὶ χαρῆναι ἔδει ὅτι ὁ ἀδελφός σου οὗτος νεκρὸς ἦν καὶ ἀνέζησεν καὶ ἀπολωλὼς ἦν, καὶ εὑρέθη
verses.king_james: It was meet that we should make merry, and be glad: for this thy brother was dead, and is alive again; and was lost, and is found.
